name: sigmoid activation function
description: The nonlinear function σ(v) = 1/(1 + e⁻ᵛ) used to introduce nonlinearity in neural networks.

name: radial basis function network
description: A neural network variant that uses Gaussian radial basis functions for the activation function σ(v).

name: softmax activation function
description: An output transformation for K-class classification that produces positive probability estimates summing to one, identical to the multilogit model.

name: hidden units
description: The middle layer units in a neural network that compute derived features Zm, whose values are not directly observed.

name: delta rule
description: Another name for the back-propagation algorithm for training neural networks.

name: batch learning
description: A training approach where parameter updates are computed as a sum over all training cases before updating.

name: online learning
description: A training approach that processes one observation at a time, updating gradients after each training case and cycling through the training set multiple times.

name: training epoch
description: One complete sweep through the entire training set in online learning.

name: stochastic approximation
description: A class of iterative methods for optimization where the learning rate decreases to zero as iterations progress, ensuring convergence.

name: conjugate gradients
description: An optimization method that avoids explicit computation of the Hessian while providing faster convergence than back-propagation.

name: variable metric methods
description: Optimization techniques that avoid explicit Hessian computation while achieving faster convergence than gradient descent.

name: hinton diagrams
description: Grayscale heat maps showing estimated neural network weights, used to visualize and interpret what features each hidden unit extracts.

name: local connectivity
description: A neural network constraint where each hidden unit connects only to a small patch of units in the layer below, reducing total parameters.

name: weight sharing
description: A constraint where multiple units in a feature map use the same set of weights, forcing extracted features to be computed by the same linear functional across different image regions.

name: feature maps
description: Arrays of hidden units in convolutional networks that share weights and extract the same type of feature across different regions of the input.

name: receptive field
description: The region of input that a hidden unit receives connections from in a locally connected network.

name: bayesian neural networks
description: Neural networks trained using Bayesian inference with MCMC methods to sample from the posterior distribution of parameters and average predictions.

name: hybrid monte carlo
description: An MCMC approach that includes an auxiliary momentum vector and implements Hamiltonian dynamics to avoid random walk behavior and achieve faster convergence.

name: automatic relevance determination
description: A Bayesian feature selection method where weights for each feature share a common prior variance, and features with small posterior variances are discarded.

name: boosted neural networks
description: Applying boosting to neural networks, typically using weak learners (small networks with few hidden units) as base learners.

name: bagged neural networks
description: Applying bootstrap aggregating (bagging) to neural networks by training multiple networks on bootstrap resamples and averaging predictions.

name: screened features
description: Features that have been filtered or selected before model training, creating a legitimate self-contained procedure.

name: ard reduced features
description: Features reduced using Automatic Relevance Determination from Bayesian neural networks, representing a data-driven dimensionality reduction.

name: neural network computational complexity
description: The computational cost of fitting neural networks, typically requiring O(NpML) operations where N is observations, p is predictors, M is hidden units, and L is training epochs.

name: support vector classifier
description: An extension of optimal separating hyperplanes to nonseparable cases where classes overlap, allowing some points on the wrong side of the margin using slack variables.

name: margin
description: The distance M from the separating hyperplane to the closest training point, where M = 1/||β||.

name: slack variables
description: Variables ξi that allow points to violate the margin constraints in support vector classifiers, measuring the degree of overlap between classes.

name: convex optimization
description: An optimization problem with quadratic criterion and linear inequality constraints that has a unique global solution.

name: nonconvex optimization
description: An optimization problem that may have multiple local minima, resulting from certain formulations like measuring overlap in actual distance from margin.

name: kernel methods
description: Techniques that produce nonlinear decision boundaries by constructing linear boundaries in transformed high-dimensional feature spaces.

name: penalization method
description: A formulation of SVMs as optimization with a penalty term, connecting SVMs to regularization approaches.

name: svm for classification
description: Support vector machine adapted for classification tasks by finding optimal decision boundaries in feature space.

name: linear decision boundary
description: A hyperplane that separates classes in the original feature space, defined by f(x) = x^T β + β0 = 0.

name: nonlinear decision boundary
description: A decision boundary that is not a hyperplane, achieved by SVMs through kernel transformations of the feature space.

name: feature space transformation
description: Mapping input features to a higher-dimensional space where linear boundaries can represent complex nonlinear patterns.

name: signed distance
description: The distance from a point x to a hyperplane, with sign indicating which side of the boundary, given by f(x) = x^T β + β0.

name: margin constraint
description: Constraints ensuring that all training points are at least distance M from the decision boundary: yi*f(xi) >= M for all i.

name: overlap penalty
description: A penalty term Σξi that measures the total amount of overlap when classes are not perfectly separable.

name: cost parameter
description: A tuning parameter C that controls the tradeoff between margin width and training error in support vector classifiers.

name: polynomial kernel
description: A kernel function of the form K(x, x') = (1 + ⟨x, x'⟩)^d that computes polynomial feature expansions efficiently.

name: radial basis kernel
description: A kernel function K(x, x') = exp(-γ||x - x'||²) that measures similarity based on Euclidean distance, also known as RBF or Gaussian kernel.

name: neural network kernel
description: A kernel function K(x, x') = tanh(κ1⟨x, x'⟩ + κ2) inspired by neural network activation functions.

name: lagrange multipliers
description: Optimization variables (αi) used to solve constrained optimization problems by converting them to dual form.

name: lagrangian primal function
description: The original formulation of the optimization problem before conversion to dual form, incorporating constraints via Lagrange multipliers.

name: inner product representation
description: A formulation where the optimization problem and solution depend on input features only through inner products, enabling the kernel trick.

name: hinge loss
description: A loss function L(y, f) = [1 - yf]+ used in SVMs that penalizes points within the margin or on the wrong side.

name: loss plus penalty formulation
description: The reformulation of SVMs as minimizing a loss function (hinge loss) plus a penalty term (regularization), connecting SVMs to other statistical methods.

name: regularization parameter
description: Parameter λ (equal to 1/C) that controls the tradeoff between model complexity and training error in the loss+penalty formulation.

name: linearly separable
description: A property of two classes when there exists a hyperplane that perfectly separates them with no overlap.

name: nonseparable case
description: A situation where two classes overlap in feature space and cannot be perfectly separated by a hyperplane.

name: svm path algorithm
description: An efficient algorithm for fitting the entire sequence of SVM models obtained by varying the cost parameter C, similar to LARS for lasso.

name: multiclass svm
description: Extension of binary SVM to multiclass problems by building classifiers for each pair of classes and using voting or multinomial loss.

name: one-versus-one classification
description: A multiclass strategy where a binary classifier is built for each pair of classes, with the final prediction based on voting.

name: overfit boundary
description: An excessively complex, wiggly decision boundary that fits training data too closely, resulting from large values of the cost parameter C.

name: smooth boundary
description: A decision boundary with low complexity achieved by small values of ||β||, controlled by a small cost parameter C.

name: tuning parameter
description: A parameter (such as C in SVMs) that controls model complexity and must be selected via methods like cross-validation.

name: epsilon-insensitive loss
description: A loss function for SVM regression that ignores errors smaller than ε, analogous to how SVMs ignore well-classified points far from the boundary.

name: svm for regression
description: Extension of support vector machines to regression problems using epsilon-insensitive loss to create a tube around the prediction function.

name: smoothing splines
description: A regression method using spline basis functions with penalty on roughness, related to projection pursuit and regularization methods.

name: radial basis function expansion
description: A basis expansion method using radial basis functions, mathematically equivalent to certain kernel methods and regularization estimates.

name: regularization estimate
description: A statistical estimation method that adds a penalty term to prevent overfitting, mathematically connected to kernel methods and basis expansions.

name: training-class separation
description: The degree to which classes can be separated in a feature space, improved by enlarging the space with basis expansions.

name: kkt conditions
description: Karush-Kuhn-Tucker optimality conditions that characterize the solution to constrained optimization problems, implying labeled points fall into distinct groups.

name: feasible point
description: A point that satisfies all constraints in an optimization problem, used to establish bounds on the objective function.

name: duality gap
description: The difference between the primal and dual objective values, which is zero at optimality for convex problems.

name: linear inequality constraints
description: Constraints of the form g(x) ≤ 0 in optimization problems, which preserve convexity when combined with a convex objective.

name: quadratic criterion
description: An objective function that is quadratic in the optimization variables, ensuring convexity and unique solutions.

name: prototype methods
description: Classification methods that represent training data by a set of points in feature space, where each prototype has an associated class label and classification is made to the class of the closest prototype.

name: model-free methods
description: Simple, highly unstructured methods for classification and pattern recognition that are not useful for understanding relationships but can be very effective as black box prediction engines.

name: k-means clustering for classification
description: Application of K-means clustering to labeled data by applying K-means separately to training data in each class, using R prototypes per class.

name: within cluster variance
description: The total variance within clusters that K-means procedure iteratively minimizes by moving cluster centers.

name: learning vector quantization
description: An online algorithm due to Kohonen where prototypes are placed strategically with respect to decision boundaries, with training points attracting prototypes of the correct class and repelling others.

name: LVQ1
description: The basic learning vector quantization procedure that moves prototypes towards training points of the same class and away from training points of different classes.

name: LVQ2
description: A modification of LVQ1 that can sometimes improve performance.

name: LVQ3
description: A modification of LVQ1 that can sometimes improve performance.

name: online algorithm
description: An algorithm where observations are processed one at a time, as in LVQ.

name: stochastic approximation learning rates
description: Guidelines for decreasing the learning rate to zero with each iteration in online learning algorithms like LVQ.

name: soft clustering
description: Clustering method where observations are assigned fractional responsibilities or weights for each cluster, as in Gaussian mixture models.

name: hard clustering
description: Clustering method where observations are assigned to exactly one cluster, as in K-means.

name: soft classification
description: Classification approach that produces smooth posterior probabilities for each class, though the final decision rule still selects the class with maximum probability.

name: k-nearest-neighbor classifier
description: A memory-based classifier that finds k training points closest to a query point and classifies using majority vote among the k neighbors.

name: memory-based classifier
description: A classifier that requires no model to be fit and uses the training data directly for prediction.

name: majority vote
description: A classification decision rule where the class is chosen based on the most common class among k nearest neighbors.

name: Cover and Hart result
description: A famous result showing that asymptotically the error rate of the 1-nearest-neighbor classifier is never more than twice the Bayes rate.

name: asymptotic nearest-neighbor error bound
description: The property that 1-nearest-neighbor error rate is bounded by twice the Bayes error rate asymptotically.

name: adaptive nearest-neighbor
description: Nearest-neighbor rules designed to alleviate the substantial bias that can occur in real problems with the standard nearest-neighbor approach.

name: tangent distance
description: An invariant distance metric for nearest-neighbor classification that explicitly incorporates natural affine invariances.

name: discriminant adaptive nearest-neighbor
description: An adaptive nearest-neighbor method that uses a local linear discriminant analysis to estimate the local between-class covariance matrix and adaptively shape the neighborhood.

name: chi-square distance
description: A distance measure for discrete distributions used in analyzing market basket data and contingency tables.

name: market basket analysis
description: Analysis of large databases of customer transactions to discover association rules showing which items are purchased together.

name: association rules
description: Rules of the form X => Y indicating that when itemset X appears in a transaction, itemset Y is also likely to appear, measured by support and confidence.

name: support
description: In association rules, the percentage of transactions containing both itemsets X and Y in the rule X => Y.

name: confidence
description: In association rules, the percentage of transactions containing X that also contain Y in the rule X => Y.

name: apriori algorithm
description: An efficient algorithm for finding frequent itemsets in market basket analysis by exploiting the downward closure property.

name: downward closure property
description: The property that if an itemset is frequent, then all of its subsets must also be frequent, exploited by the Apriori algorithm.

name: frequent itemset
description: An itemset that appears in at least a minimum support threshold of transactions in market basket analysis.
